source_pr.title: planner,executor: fix join resolveIndex won't find its column from children schema & amend join's lused and rused logic for reversed column ref from join schema to its children
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/42588

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?
issue https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/42588 is caused by many factors and this pr is try to resolve them all.
* first: resolve parent pointer modification error caused by the apply elimination, as mentioned here https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/42588#issuecomment-1929026146
* second: since TiDB internal has many non-rigorous logical optimizing rules, occasionally two or more of them will leading a critical assumption which will lead a resolveIndex error as mentioned here: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/45831#discussion_r1481031471
* third: in the execution of join, the inline projection's `lused` and `rused` are order unawareness, which means even if we  allow reversed column ref in join schema itself to its children, it will break its assumption here too. So we need to fix here as well.
example: 
```
join schema[col11, col22, col21], child1 [col1], child2 [col21, col22]
the pull/45831 make an assumption here that join schema won't refer a column-order revered from the order of its children. Like here [col22, col21] in join schema is reversed with  [col21, col22] from its child2. Step2 try to fix this.

in the execution layer.
join schema[col11, col22, col21], child1 [col1], child2 [col21, col22]
the lused will be [true]
the rused will be [true, true]
when constructing the join row result, the order couldn't be restored from a simple bool array. Consequently one join result will be shown like: <col1, col21, col22> while the field type is totally different from what the datum in the same position. So step3 try to fix this.
```

- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory

Documentation

- [ ] Contains variable changes

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: slices.SortFunc(usedIdxPair, func(a, b intPair) int { (pkg/executor/benchmark_test.go)
